channel.title: Comments for page "Letters from Benares"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Letters from Benares"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Have studied Hindi for quite some time now, and you're correct in your assumptions. :)

Also, not entirely unrelated: I like your writing! Good job!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I haven't done a lot of research into the point, but I suspect that "Raj" was one of the Hindi words that was incorporated very quickly into English as part of the British rule in India (there are lots of others). Violet is generally very enthusiastic about the adventure of living in Benares, and you'll notice that even in her first letter, she has adopted a number of other local words (lassi, Maharajah, sadhu, guru), so I don't think it's out of the question for her to use Raj.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
British Raj is the correct English term to refer to the British Empire in India.

Edit: In fact, "Indian Empire" means something completely different from "British Empire in India" and would never be a correct way to refer to such.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My, I almost forgot.

Here is the translation! Thank you for your well-written story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "British Raj" means "regime of Britain" in Hindis language. It is strange for person of 1909 to use that expression in English. I think it should be modified into "Indian Empire".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Author here. MrWrong is entirely correct - M-F stands for "Militant-Faithful". Cousin Emily turns out to be part of the CotBG, and the most badass character I've ever written!

I'm very excited about a Korean translation - thanks for your interest. Let me know when it's done, so I can link to it from my author page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not the author here. But if I recall correctly, "M-F" stands for Militant-Faithful (the self-defence group for Cogwork Orthodox Church). For more information: http://www.scp-wiki.net/church-of-the-broken-god-hub

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Translator from the Korean Wiki. What does the word "M-F" stand for?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this, but I feel it ended a bit abruptly.

Tragic housefires tend to be a bit abrupt, I guess. Presumably Miss Cavanagh didn't want this to grow too much longer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this, but I feel it ended a bit abruptly. It would have been fun to read about more sarkic yoga.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Link will suffice. I always feel parent pages to be best reserved for supplements.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
psul, since this is cast as "additional materials" on SCP-2833, may I link this from my article?

Absolutely - you're welcome to put a link and/or make that the parent page of this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I barely remember 2833, but it doesn't matter, because this is just excellently written. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It makes sense:) One of the many influences Sarkicism had was House Dagoth of Elder Scrolls: Morrowind. Which includes some rather severe facial augmentations http://img13.deviantart.net/ca70/i/2014/001/f/b/ash_ghoul_dagoth_by_victoriadaedra-d70drsz.jpg

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I saw a man who looked like he had a long elephant's trunk, and I asked Guru if it was Ganesha, but he just laughed at me and told me that they didn't worship Ganesha in this temple.

A Sarkicist with an elephant trunk, I'm calling it that!

Anyway, my headcanon of this is that Guru is exceptionally smart for an SCP-2833 instance, and tries to target the ruling British to expand its influence (a la Dracula).

EDIT: psul, since this is cast as "additional materials" on SCP-2833, may I link this from my article?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Originally drafted for a Christmas Exchange gift for MrWrong, and added here with his kind permission.

Many thanks for your review and assistance to MrWrong, Metaphysician, sirpudding, Dora, minmin and others who I may have forgotten.

Sarkic yoga is the next big thing, you know. Can't you just imagine what marvellous shape Violet and the family are in?
